concept: Brush / Painting Tool / To Apply with Strokes
description: A tool with bristles for painting, cleaning, or grooming; to apply or sweep with strokes. 'Phuri' names what flows color—the soft tool that leaves marks of intention.
sound_symbolism: The word sweeps soft. 'Phu' /φu/ breathes with gentle gesture, bristles touching surface. Then 'ri' runs in continuous stroke, color following.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo phuri' (the brush). As verb: 'mi phuri' (I brush). For painting, calligraphy, grooming—any bristled application.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The brush as voice—flowing lines, organic forms, the artist's gesture made visible.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Brush as extension of hand—calligraphy, painting, the ancient mark-making tool.
